title: Citrus Production, Pahlavi-era Bridge Segment (1950-1958)
notes: Bridges 1950-1958 WB table (Oranges&Tangerines combined, Other Citrus separate) with FAOSTAT's 1961-2024 series via one 1961 anchor row per FAOSTAT category. Honest caveat preserved from source README: WB's 1958 combined total (110,000t) is noticeably higher than FAOSTAT's 1961 Citrus Fruit Total (78,900t) three years later -- could be real decline, definitional difference, or revision; not resolved, present as two clearly-labeled distinct segments, never force-spliced into one line. For full 1961-2024 FAOSTAT detail, join against agriculture_qcl_production.csv directly.

title: CBI Annual Review — Liquidity (M2), Monetary Base & CPI Growth, FY1379-1401 (2000/01-2022/23)
notes: Native CBI Annual Review (English edition) fiscal-year series -- full 23/23-year Liquidity(M2) level + growth coverage (WDI's Broad Money for Iran runs only 1960-2016 and stops before this file's 2017-2023 tail), plus bundled monetary-base growth (19/23 yrs), monetary-base level (5/23 yrs), and CPI/inflation growth (6/23 yrs) -- all extracted directly from each year's own report narrative, never a later year's retrospective restatement. Retrieved via Wayback (cbi.ir itself is F5/TSPD bot-blocked from this project's IP). One raw-filename mislabeling (cbi_annual_review_1396_2016-17.pdf is actually FY1396=2017/18) is documented in the folder README and NOT corrected in the raw filename per bookkeeping.md immutability rule -- this project's own fiscal_year_ah field is authoritative. Units: billion rials FY1379-1393, converted from trillion rials (x1000) FY1394-1401 for consistency.

title: Iran Air / Iranian Civil Aviation — Passengers, Employees & Revenue (1950-2003)
notes: WDI's IS.AIR.PSGR (air transport passengers) only starts in 1970 (715,600, Iran-wide) -- this file fills the pre-1970 gap back to 1950 (Iran Air passengers ~142,000 in 1961) plus employee/revenue figures WDI never tracks. 1972 Iran Air figure (~800,000) roughly cross-checks against WDI's 1972 Iran-wide figure (894,800) since Iran Air was the dominant carrier. Tertiary business-reference source (Encyclopedia.com company history), flagged order-of-magnitude reliable per bookkeeping.md, not an exact primary annual report.

title: Iran Air Founding, Fleet Size, Workforce & Major Accidents (1952-1988)
notes: No existing chart tracks airline fleet size, workforce, or aviation accidents for Iran. Workforce grew 700 (1962) to 12,000+ (1978/79); fleet reached 35 all-jet aircraft carrying ~5 million passengers/year by approx. 1978 (year is imprecise in source, flagged as such); starkest single data point is the 1988 Iran Air Flight 655 shootdown (290 deaths). Encyclopaedia Iranica narrative source, retrieved via interactive browser tool (iranicaonline.org blocks curl/WebFetch, HTTP 403).

title: Iran Inflation Rate (CBI/Bank Melli-sourced), Pre-WDI Extension (1937-1959)
time_range: 1937-2014 (WDI FP.CPI.TOTL.ZG for Iran starts 1960; 1937-1959 is the genuine gap-fill)
notes: Central Bank of Iran-sourced annual inflation-rate series via Iran Data Portal (Syracuse), 78 continuous years. This is the exact series named as this project's designated pre-1960 CPI/deflator source in docs/bookkeeping.md's currency-conversion methodology section -- already load-bearing for the project's real/USD conversion pipeline even though it was never itself given a chart_id. Full 1937-2014 span kept as one continuous native series (not split at 1960) since it is methodologically consistent throughout, unlike the WDI series it extends.

title: Employment by Sector, 14-Sector Breakdown, Absolute Headcounts (1956-2011)
notes: Statistical Centre of Iran, via Iran Data Portal. Absolute headcount levels across 14 sectors (agriculture, oil, mining, industry, construction, trade, services split multiple ways, uncategorized) -- structurally distinct from WDI's SL.AGR/IND/SRV.EMPL.ZS (which are % shares, only 3 sectors, and only start 1991 for Iran as modeled ILO estimates). One of the two deepest continuous Iran-specific economic series in the database outside Maddison's reconstruction (per folder README), spanning nearly the entire Second Pahlavi era plus the full Islamic Republic period unbroken. Kept as its own chart rather than an alt-source footnote given this depth and structural distinctness.

title: Employment by Gender, Absolute Headcounts & Female Share (1966-2011)
notes: Management and Planning Organization of Iran, via Iran Data Portal. Employment LEVELS (male/female/total headcount + female share %), distinct from WDI's labor-force-participation-RATE charts (SL.TLF.CACT.FE etc, which are rates of the working-age population, not employed headcounts).

title: Housing Units by Number of Resident Households, Census Years (1966-2006)
notes: Statistical Centre of Iran. No existing chart_id tracks housing-unit counts for Iran at any date (WDI has no housing-stock indicator with Iran coverage). Total housing units + breakdown by 1/2/3/4+ households per unit (housing-density proxy). New category 'Housing' introduced since none of the existing registry categories fit.

title: Labor Force by Occupation Category x Gender x Urban/Rural (2005-2014)
time_range: 2005-2014 (2005-2013 portion is a content-duplicate of the target chart's own source; 2014-2015 is the sole genuinely new year)
notes: IMPORTANT DEDUP FINDING from this audit: this file's 2005-2013 rows are near-identical (same SCI/MCLS source, same 9 area x gender group values, e.g. 20,618,579 total employed 2005-06) to the ALREADY-REGISTERED iran_disasters_regional_series/labor_force_by_occupation_and_gender_2005_2013.csv -- both trace to the same Iran Data Portal SCI table, just harmonized independently in two different batches under two different processed folders. Registered here as 'extends' rather than a duplicate 'new' chart specifically to avoid double-counting; the only genuinely new content is the 2014-2015 year this version has and the registered one doesn't. Flagged for a future folder-level dedup pass between iran_data_portal_deep_series and iran_disasters_regional_series (out of this audit's scope, which is folder-completeness, not cross-folder dedup).

title: Labor Force Participation Rate by Gender x Urban/Rural, Native SCI Series (2005-2014)
notes: Native SCI/MCLS-sourced participation rate with urban/rural x gender breakdown that WDI's modeled-ILO-estimate series does not have -- kept as an alt-source line on the same chart concept per this project's 'both sources, never pick a winner' rule. DEDUP NOTE from this audit: a content-duplicate unregistered file also exists at iran_disasters_regional_series/labor_force_participation_rate_2005_2014.csv (same values, e.g. 41%/64.7%/17% national/men/women 2005-06) -- that sibling file is itself NOT yet in CHART_REGISTRY.csv or any staging file, which is outside this audit's assigned folder list; flagged here for a future pass, not fixed in this one to avoid scope creep beyond the assigned candidate folders.

title: 1901 D'Arcy Oil Concession — Contract Terms
time_range: 1901 (single contract's terms, 17 rows, not a time series)
notes: Origin of the Anglo-Persian/Anglo-Iranian/BP lineage: signing date, 60-year duration, ~1,242,000 sq km territory, GBP20,000 upfront cash + GBP20,000 in shares, royalty structure. Contract TERMS as signed, not a performance/output series -- does not show actual oil produced or royalties actually paid (that belongs to pahlavi_government_finance_series's oil-revenue tables and the separate USBM AIOC-profits/royalties extraction). New category 'Foreign Concessions & Industrial Licensing' introduced; no existing registry category fits concession-terms-as-signed data.

title: Pre-1979 Automotive Foreign Licensing/Assembly Joint Ventures
notes: Jeep Iran Trading Co. (Willys-Overland/Kaiser 1956), Iran National/Rootes-Chrysler-Peugeot Paykan licence (1966-2005, 2.2M+ units), Iran National/Daimler-Benz bus-truck licence (1966-1979), Khawar Industrial Group/Daimler-Benz truck licence (1966, merged into Iran Khodro Diesel 1999), General Motors Iran Ltd. (1972-1980). Institutional venture-structure facts (partners, licence dates), NOT production volumes -- distinct metric type from the already-registered iran_automotive_national_vehicle_production_1970_2018 chart (manufacturing OUTPUT COUNTS from a different source, iran_automotive_textile_series), so not staged as extends of that chart. A raw CSV-quoting defect (unescaped comma in one foreign_partner field) was fixed on the way into data/processed per bookkeeping.md; raw file left untouched.

title: Cinema: Movie-House Counts & Audience Attendance (1932-1986)
notes: No existing chart tracks cinema infrastructure for Iran. Sharp boom-then-bust story from official Iranian statistics: 142 movie houses (1959) -> 453 (1975) -> 198 (1979, ~255 cinemas burned/closed in the two years before the revolution) -> partial recovery to 247 (1986). New category 'Media & Culture' introduced. Encyclopaedia Iranica source, retrieved via interactive browser tool (iranicaonline.org blocks curl/WebFetch, HTTP 403).

title: Film Production, Distribution & Censorship (1941-1982)
notes: Films screened/produced/distributed by origin country, production-company spend, censorship (films inspected/banned/rejected). Full-length film production rose 25->80 films/year (1959-1971) before a 1979 censorship purge banned 1,800 of 2,000 films inspected that year.

title: Newspaper & Periodical Counts, Adult Literacy by Gender (1898-1988)
notes: Daily/weekly/monthly newspaper and periodical counts: 198 papers (1976) -> 237 (1979-80) -> 163 (1988), plus a 1976 adult-literacy-by-gender data point. Topic-scoped extract of the same Iranica 'COMMUNICATIONS in Persia' article used for iran_telecom_communications_series (postal/telegraph/telephone/radio/TV) -- full article text stored once, not duplicated, per that folder's SOURCE_NOTE.

title: Postal Service: Post Office/Box Counts & Mail Volumes (1913-1989)
notes: No existing chart tracks Iran's postal infrastructure at any date. Encyclopaedia Iranica 'COMMUNICATIONS in Persia' (Sreberny-Mohammadi & Mohammadi), sourced to Iran Statistical Yearbook editions where the article itself cites them.

title: Telegraph: Cable Length, Office Counts & Telex Stations (1914-1989)
notes: No existing chart tracks telegraph infrastructure for Iran (WDI has no telegraph indicator at any date). This is the richest pre-1990 telecom source in the database, filling a gap back to 1858 across the sibling files in this folder.

title: Telephone Subscribers, Pre-WDI Extension (1914-1988)
notes: WDI's IT.MLT.MAIN (fixed telephone subscriptions) only starts in 1960 with a flat, repeated 116,417 figure for Iran -- this file fills the gap back to 1914 plus richer detail (public/private counts, waiting list, direct-dial share) WDI never tracked at any date.

title: Radio Set Ownership & Television Founding/Audience Reach (1940-1989)
notes: No existing chart tracks radio/TV ownership or reach for Iran at any date. Radio-set ownership (urban/rural split) from 1924 groundwork through 1989; TV founding events (1958) and audience-reach figures. Not perfectly reconciled with a related 1978 TV-set figure in the sibling iran_media_history_series/cinema_theaters_and_attendance.csv notes -- both kept as printed per this project's no-silent-correction rule.

title: Literacy Corps (Sepah-e Danesh) — Cumulative Program Totals (1963-1979)
notes: Corpsmen/corpswomen counts, children/adults taught, corps pay rates for the 1963 White Revolution's rural literacy-mobilization program. No existing chart tracks this. period_start/period_end schema (not a single-year column) since figures are cumulative totals across the whole program.

title: Illiteracy Rate by Sex, Age 15+, Pre-WDI Extension (1958-1979)
notes: Sourced to UNESCO Statistical Yearbook 1966 + an uncited 1979 figure: illiteracy 67.2% male / 87.8% female (1966) -> 44.2% male / 53.0% female (1979). DOES NOT RECONCILE with a separate literacy claim in the sibling white_revolution_corps_stats.csv (26%->42% national literacy 1963-1978, explicitly marked 'unverified' in its Wikipedia source) -- both kept unreconciled per this project's no-fabrication rule; see that chart's own notes.

title: Government Health Expenditure vs. Total Expenditure & Oil Revenue, Pre-WDI Extension (1920-1978)
time_range: 1920-1978 (WDI SH.XPD.GHED.GD.ZS for Iran starts 2000 -- fully non-overlapping gap-fill)
notes: Also includes immunization coverage figures. Internal ambiguity preserved not resolved: source gives both a '$0.5bn single-year (1974)' figure and a '$1.5bn->$116.5bn cumulative 1948-74 growth' framing without reconciling the two -- both rows kept, flagged in their own notes. Iranica's own Tables 1-3 in the source BEHDARI article (health worker/facility counts 1923-1978) are print-plate images, not machine-readable -- could not be extracted, flagged as a genuine gap not fabricated around.

title: Extension & Development Corps (Sepah-e Tarvij) — Service Structure & Fertilizer Adoption
time_range: 1964-1965 (+2003 comparison rows, explicitly modern-era, kept only as before/after scale check)
notes: GFRAS (Global Forum for Rural Advisory Services) Iran country page. New category 'Social Programs & Rural Development' introduced for the White Revolution corps folder's institutional/program data that doesn't fit Education/Health.

title: White Revolution Corps Bundle: Health Treatment Totals, Ag Production Change, Enrollment, Land Reform (1963-1978)
notes: LOWER-CONFIDENCE SOURCE, flagged distinctly: retrieved via Wikipedia raw wikitext specifically to preserve inline <ref> markers, but several rows (education-enrollment-by-level, the 26%->42% national literacy claim, the 1.5-million-families land-reform figure) are explicitly marked 'unverified' in the current Wikipedia article with no inline citation. Kept per this project's transparency-over-omission rule, not treated as authoritative without independent corroboration. Bundled as one chart (Health Corps treatment totals, agricultural production change, literacy rate, school enrollment by level, land-reform beneficiary families) rather than split into 5 charts given the shared low-confidence flag and small size; period_start/period_end + corps schema.

title: Iran Provincial Poverty Rates by Macro-Region (2011 vs. 2020)
notes: World Bank Islamic Republic of Iran Poverty Assessment (Nov 2023), based on Iran's own Household Income and Expenditure Survey (HIES). No existing chart_id tracks a poverty RATE for Iran at any date (WDI's SI.POV.NAHC has zero Iran coverage; SI.POV.GINI is inequality, a different concept). National poverty rate rose 20% (2011) to 28% (2020); 8 macro-regions, digitized from the report's Figure 24 chart (not a printed table) -- accurate to the nearest whole percentage point only. Figures 25-26 (31-province-level map + correlation plot) remain undigitized, a natural future deepening, not attempted this round.

title: Tobacco Monopoly: Regie Concession, Dokhaniyat Conversion & Workforce (1890-1995)
notes: 1890 Tobacco Regie concession terms (the 1891-92 protest that ended it -- UK Parliament Hansard source), 1929 Dokhaniyat state-monopoly conversion, 1940 cultivation/output (pre-FAOSTAT, which starts 1961), 1967 workforce (750,000), 1995 factory workforce. Institutional/monopoly-structure history, not a clean production-quantity series -- NOT staged as extends of faostat__Unmanufactured tobacco__production despite the topical overlap, since most rows are concession/workforce facts rather than production figures. Also distinct from the already-registered iran_cigarette_production_1954_1959 chart, whose own notes explicitly flag this file as covering tobacco LEAF/cultivation+monopoly-structure, 'not manufactured cigarettes' -- a different product stage, confirmed not a splice candidate for that chart either.

title: Tobacco Market Post-2012-Privatization: Consumption, Smokers & Import Dependency (2018)
notes: Post-2012-privatization consumption, active smokers, import dependency (80% of leaf imported), Iranian Tobacco Company's collapsed market share (~17.5%) vs. Japan Tobacco International (>50%). Tobacco Asia trade press source.

title: Iran Handwoven Carpet Export Value (1960-2024) — Incl. the $2.13bn 1994 Peak to Post-JCPOA Collapse
notes: ONE OF THIS DATABASE'S HEADLINE STORIES -- explicitly confirmed to have no prior chart_id by this audit. All-time peak $2.132bn (1994) collapsing to tens of millions USD (2024, post-JCPOA-withdrawal). 1960-1986: export value (rials/USD), Iran Carpet Company's state-monopoly output share; flags a likely million/billion-rial unit error in the printed source for 1982-83 (kept as printed, not corrected). 1994-2024: compiled from news reporting + UN Comtrade via tradingeconomics.com; one Tehran-Times-sourced row (2024) labeled state-media-attributed per bookkeeping.md rule 4, no independent corroboration found for that specific figure; two sub-annual rows (Q2 2020, 11-month 1401/1400) flagged, do not chart alongside full-year totals without labeling. CROSS-REFERENCE (not spliced, kept as two separate chart concepts): the already-registered pahlavi__exports_by_commodity_value_1956_59 chart's own notes flag its 'Carpets' row (1956/57-1958/59, one row within a 14-commodity table) as sitting immediately before this chart's 1960 start -- a real 3-year splice opportunity for a future pass, not performed here since merging a single-commodity 64-year series into a 14-commodity 3-year table's alt_sources would conflate two structurally different chart shapes; flagged in both directions instead.

title: CITES Caviar Export Quota, Iran (2006) — Source Record for the Registered Timeline's 2006 Row
notes: CONFIRMED DUPLICATE CONTENT, found during this audit: this file's single row (44,370 kg, UN News/CITES Secretariat) is the IDENTICAL figure already present as the 2006 row inside the ALREADY-REGISTERED iran_trade__cites_caviar_quota_trade_timeline_1998_2006 chart's own underlying file (iran_trade_institutions_fx_series/cites_caviar_quota_trade_timeline_1998_2006.csv), whose own row explicitly says 'the same figure already captured in this project's Round 35 finding... reproduced here for continuity.' Registered as extends (not a new duplicate chart) specifically to resolve the ambiguity that chart's own notes flagged: 'that folder is outside this task's scope and has not itself been registered... flagged here as a genuine ambiguity for whichever future pass registers specialty_goods_series' -- this audit is that pass.

title: Official Caviar Production & Export (Shilat / Iran Fisheries Organization) (2013-2024)
notes: Iran Fisheries Organization (Shilat) official caviar production/export/sturgeon-meat tonnage -- distinct from the CITES quota chart (regulatory ceiling vs. actual reported output, roughly a decade apart). Three Tehran-Times-sourced rows labeled state-media-attributed (on-the-record quotes from a named Shilat official, not anonymous). New category 'Fisheries & Aquaculture' introduced.

title: Sturgeon Aquaculture Production, Whole-Fish Live Weight (2010-2018)
notes: EUMOFA (European Commission DG MARE) 'The Caviar Market -- 2021 Edition,' sourced to FAO. Iran's pivot from wild-catch to farming -- whole-fish live weight, NOT extracted caviar/roe weight (roe is typically ~10-15% of a mature female sturgeon's weight), not directly comparable tonne-for-tonne to the Shilat caviar-specific figures. Table visually verified via pdftoppm render against raw PDF text (European '.'-as-thousands-separator formatting could otherwise be misread). Only Iran's column extracted from a report that also has China/Russia/USA/Vietnam/Armenia/EU columns, per this project's Iran-first rule.

title: Sugar: Consumption, Import Origins & State-Monopoly History, Companion Series (1890-2000)
notes: The target chart's own alt_sources note already flagged this file as '(not yet registry-staged)' -- this audit resolves that. Broader companion to the target's narrow 1954-1959 refined-sugar-OUTPUT-level series: Tehran consumption, sugar-import-value-by-country-of-origin (Russia/France/Belgium), 1932 state-monopoly founding, 1970s-80s consumption growth, year-2000 ISO ranking -- different metrics per row, not a strict same-axis continuation, so treat as a multi-metric companion series on the same chart rather than a clean splice. One preserved-not-corrected arithmetic discrepancy (1906/07 country-breakdown rows sum ~0.3% over the printed total) and one year-typo correction ('1656-57'->1956) documented in the folder README.

title: Tea: Cultivation Buildout, State Organization & Production, Companion Series (1895-1984)
notes: The target chart's own alt_sources note already flagged this file as '(not yet registry-staged), which covers the same processed-tea-industry topic across a wider 1895-1984 span' -- this audit resolves that. Pre-cultivation import era, cultivation-area buildout (100 ha 1920 -> 24,000+ ha 1968), state Iran Tea Organization/Company founding, production/import ranges -- broader companion to the target's narrow 1954-1959 refined-output-level series.

title: Trans-Iranian Railway Cumulative Financing Cost, Fiscal Context for the Sugar/Tea Excise Tax
notes: Cumulative Trans-Iranian Railway cost (Wikipedia, citing Mikiya Koyagi's research) -- fiscal context for the 1925 sugar/tea excise tax that substantially financed the railway's construction. Total project COST, not tax revenue -- a year-by-year or commodity-disaggregated sugar/tea excise REVENUE series was specifically sought and not found (logged as a real gap in the raw manifest, a Majlis budget-law-archive lead is noted for a future pass). Distinct metric from the already-registered iran_railways_income_expense_1953_1961 chart (operating income/expense, 1953-61, not construction cost, and 15+ years later) -- not staged as extends of that chart given the metric and era mismatch; same category reused for topical consistency.
